knowledge.insight: Robertson: On TVA (Tennessee Valley Authority) lakes in summer, you have no choice but to get off the bank. Fish the offshore ledges — that's where the bass live.

Robertson: On offshore ledges, you won't get a lot of bites, but there are little things you can do to trigger bigger bites that other anglers aren't doing — subtle presentation adjustments make the difference.
Robertson: Don't avoid community holes on ledge fisheries — let other anglers fish through first, then work behind them. But save your best weight-producing spots where you won't have competition.

Robertson: A lifetime of experience on Tennessee River ledge fisheries (Kentucky Lake, Pickwick) is invaluable — knowing how fish set up on ledges from years of fishing them gives you an edge others can't replicate.
